sutta: monks possess the 5 qualities among practicing awareness in breathing in and breathing out will in no long time penetrate the immovable of what 5 he is set on little with few duties easily supported well content with life's necessities taking food in little he serves not his own belly slothful and little he is heat full in wakefulness he is much learned with a retentive and well stored mind those teachings lovely in the beginning lovely in the middle and lovely in the end which sets forth in spirit and letter the holy life of purity perfect in its entirety those teachings are much heard by him remembered verbally recited carefully pondered over in mind fully understood in theory and he reflects on the mind as freed monks possess of these 5 qualities among practicing awareness in breathing in and breathing out will in no long time penetrate the immovable that's the end of the suta
commentary: so here this with us is that when we practice mindfulness of the breath and these few things are very helpful first 1 he has few duties and he's easily supported contented second 1 he takes uh little food like moderate food now and let's not overeat because when you eat too much we become sleepy it's difficult to meditate third 1 is heat full and wakefulness that means does not sleep too much tries to maintain wakefulness the fourth 1 he's much learned as much learner is stressed quite often that means uh we are much learned in the sutras refers to the discourses of the buddha we have much knowledge of these discourses of the buddha then the fifth 1 he reflects on the mind as freedom so these 5 qualities are helpful to obtain liberation and exercise

items: reflects on the mind as freed
